ontology.foodex2_description: Croissant — laminated butter pastry made from enriched wheat flour dough
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for croissant as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for individual pastry products is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard butter croissant weighing approximately 60 g, as typically served in a Singapore café or bakery.

nutrient_source.title: French T45 Butter Croissant — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (G00011005)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values are taken directly from the HPB Foodo record G00011005 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for croissant (FDC ID 174987) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Croissant, plain — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (Atkinson et al., 2008; Foster-Powell et al., 2002)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 67 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain butter croissant (glucose reference = 100). The T45 flour variant is not separately listed; the plain croissant entry is the closest defensible analogue. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 67 × 45.8 × 0.60 / 100 = 18.4 (serving size 60 g, carbohydrate contribution 27.48 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100g combined with high sugar (11.26 g/100g) and low potassium (118 mg/100g) creates an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio; saturated fat load promotes arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit butter croissant consumption to occasional small portions, avoid pairing with salty fillings or spreads, and prioritise potassium-rich foods at the same meal to partially offset the sodium burden.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (45.8 g/100g) from T45 flour with GI ~67 and 11.26 g/100g added sugar produces a high glycaemic load per serving (~18.4); saturated fat from butter may further impair insulin sensitivity.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this croissant as an occasional indulgence only, limit portion to half a croissant (~30 g), pair with a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response, and monitor post-meal blood glucose carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of 11.5 g/100g and trans fat of 0.1 g/100g from heavy butter lamination significantly exceed recommended limits; cholesterol at 67 mg/100g and sodium at 300 mg/100g add further cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of butter croissants; if consumed, limit to one small croissant (≤60 g) infrequently and balance the meal with vegetables, legumes, and unsaturated fat sources.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~11.5 g/100g from butter lamination exceeds the 5 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold; trans fat 0.1 g/100g is pro-inflammatory at any amount; no meaningful anti-inflammatory components (omega-3, polyphenols, fibre >3 g) are present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise butter croissant intake as the high saturated and trans fat content from butter lamination promotes systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The French T45 Butter Croissant is a classic laminated pastry originating from French baking tradition, made with T45 patent wheat flour — a fine, low-ash French flour prized for its delicate crumb structure. The dough is enriched with butter, fine sugar, salt, eggs, and milk powder, then repeatedly folded with generous layers of butter to create the characteristic flaky, honeycomb interior and deeply golden, crisp exterior shell. When baked, the natural butter melds with the wheat aroma of the T45 flour to produce a rich, buttery fragrance and a melt-in-the-mouth texture. In Singapore, this style of artisan croissant is sold in premium bakeries and café chains and is typically consumed as a breakfast or snack item. Nutritionally, each 100 g delivers 406 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 21 g fat (of which approximately 11.5 g is saturated fat from butter), and 45.8 g carbohydrate with 11.26 g sugar and only 2.6 g dietary fibre. The glycaemic index is approximately 67 (medium), yielding a glycaemic load of about 18 per standard 60 g serving. Sodium is 300 mg/100g. Due to its high saturated fat, refined carbohydrate, and moderate sodium content, this croissant is a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is best consumed occasionally and in small portions, ideally paired with a protein source to moderate the glycaemic response.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a French croissant?
Is croissant high in saturated fat?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients and micronutrients are sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database record G00011005 with a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from the USDA FoodData Central croissant entry (FDC ID 174987) as the closest approved-source analogue, which reduces overall confidence to high (rather than exact) for those specific fields. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database as an analogous estimate for plain butter croissant.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (11.5 g/100g), monounsaturated fat (5.6 g/100g), polyunsaturated fat (1.2 g/100g), and trans fat (0.1 g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central, FDC ID 174987 (Croissant, butter), as HPB Foodo record G00011005 does not report fatty acid breakdown. These values are consistent with the known composition of heavily butter-laminated pastry. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB or USDA for this specific product): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Detailed vitamin and mineral panel beyond those reported by HPB is not available in the HPB Foodo database for this record, and the USDA FDC entry for croissant does not provide a complete micronutrient panel matching this specific T45 formulation. 3. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain butter croissant; the T45-specific variant is not separately listed. 4. The input record incorrectly classified this product under primary_cuisine: Chinese and ethnic_tags: Chinese. This has been corrected to French / Western Bakery as the product is a French laminated pastry sold in Singapore. Multilingual aliases are provided in English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi consistent with Singapore retail context. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the input record does not assert halal certification, so is_halal is set to false pending verification.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected primary_cuisine from 'Chinese' to 'French / Western Bakery' based on product identity.
Added Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases for Singapore retail context.
Estimated saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g from USDA FDC 174987 as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; documented in notes and nutrient_source.
Added GI of 67 from University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate for plain butter croissant); calculated glycemic_load_per_serving 18.4 for 60 g serving.
Populated all three required health_context conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) with concern_level, key_factor, and clinical guidance.
Documented all -1 fields in notes per g3 negative sentinel value rule.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency per q8 aliases consistency rule.
Added FoodOn and FoodEx2 ontology anchors; SNOMED CT null with gap note per g5 ontology honesty rule.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
